rows.notes: Clear centred page title at top. Handwriting legible for the main words 'Recepta Anni Presentis' though spelling is archaic; no monetary columns clearly aligned to this line.

Text 'a pag. sup.' visible to the right of the title; the amounts '36 7 09' are neatly aligned in the three rightmost columns (pounds 36, shillings 7, pence 9). Columns are unambiguous and each value is within valid ranges.
First item in the left-hand list beneath the title. Handwriting of the place/name is partially faded and unusual (I read 'Soukesrington Gent'); no amounts are written on this line.

Second line in the parish/person list. I read 'Carlington Aug.m Robt' (abbreviation 'Aug.m' likely stands for a name); no amount in the right columns for this line. Handwriting moderately clear but some abbreviations.
Single-word place/name in the list. Reading uncertain (appears as 'Felthsum' or similar). No amounts on this line.
Handwritten name appears to read 'Wiston Bricorage' (uncertain spelling of last word). No monetary values on this line.
Three comma-separated names/places visible. Handwriting fairly clear for these three items; no amounts shown for this row.
Reads as 'Madenham' followed by a second word that appears to be 'ffose' or 'fforse'; lettering is slightly faded. No monetary columns filled on this line.
Left-hand list entry; reads 'Backgate Major Bleay' (surname uncertain). No amounts present on this line.
Text appears to be an abbreviated phrase 'part 4t: Guins of Addenham' (very uncertain reading of the middle words). No amounts on this line; ink shadow from a fold affects clarity.

A subtotal aligned to the right of the parish list. The three columns read clearly as 63 (pounds), 3 (shillings), 4 (pence). No descriptive text on the same horizontal level; likely a subtotal for the preceding list.

Large total line beneath a ruled separator. The left two columns read '99' and '10' clearly. The pence column looks like '4' followed by a small loop-mark that resembles the common halfpenny 'ob' flourish; I interpret this as 4½d rather than '49' pence (which would be invalid). Reading of the loop/fraction is moderately uncertain due to scribble.
Small marginal note appearing under the section divider; reads 'More: Sr Batt:' (very uncertain). No amounts on this line; ink is faint and partially obscured by a shadow.

rows.description: Rec: of Mr Gariland or Mr Stubbs for fynger ending at Mr Thos 1718
rows.notes: Transaction line referencing receipts from named individuals with year '1718'. The amount '22 0 2' is clearly placed in the three rightmost columns and each value is within valid ranges. Handwriting for names is moderately legible.

rows.description: Do: of sd Throne for midwife 1719
rows.notes: Reads as continuation 'Do: of sd Throne for midwife 1719'. The pounds and shillings '45 9' are clear. The pence column appears as '5' with a small loop/mark after it (similar to earlier halfpenny flourish), interpreted as 5½d. The fraction interpretation is somewhat uncertain due to ink stroke.
Line reads 'Gent Com: Francis Bigyan' (surname slightly ambiguous but legible). Amount '8 0 0' is neatly written in the three columns and unambiguous.
Label 'Act Suppor' (Act. Support) is clearly written with a brace grouping names to its right. The amount is a round '10 0 0' in the monetary columns and clearly legible.
First name in the brace under 'Act Suppor'. The name 'Mr Warren' is legible; no separate amount is listed on this line (it's a sub-item under the bracketed total).
Sub-item inside the brace: 'Easton 2' (the trailing '2' is part of the list notation). No monetary columns filled for this sub-item; handwriting fairly legible.
Another name in the bracketed group; reads 'Harvig 2' (surname uncertain). No amounts present for the sub-item.
Sub-item in the Act Support brace. I read the name as 'Burlafe' (uncertain spelling). No monetary values on the line.
Another sub-item; looks like 'Harry' followed by an uncertain flourish or small mark. The text is cramped and partially obscured by a shadow; no amounts shown.
Sub-item 'Coode 2' under the Act Support brace. Legible; no monetary values on this line.
Final name in the bracketed group appears as 'Matthew 2'. No separate amount shown; likely part of the Act Support aggregate.
Line reads 'Com: Library' (Committee Library). The amount '4 15 0' aligned to the right is clear: 4 pounds, 15 shillings, 0 pence.
Final total at bottom-right of the page reads '94 4 09' (pounds 94, shillings 4, pence 9). Numbers are clear though the bottom of the page has a shadow; all values are in valid ranges.
